plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and align with single name caps.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to stay within conservative single name limits.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into a new sector with a strong scanner setup.
This plan aims for capital preservation, reducing overall risk and leverage.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% monthly return, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expect limited upside potential but enhanced stability.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and align with balanced single name limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure to zero.
Trim QQQI to 15% to balance income with growth potential.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a quality tech name with an uptrend.
Buy RTX at 10% and AMAT at 10% to diversify into strong scanner setups.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% monthly growth by blending broad index with quality tech. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding new, strong setups. Expect moderate risk and potential drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration while maintaining significant broad exposure.
Trim SOXL to 5% to keep some leveraged semiconductor exposure, selling SPAL.
Sell QQQI to focus on growth opportunities over income.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a high-scoring, uptrending tech name.
Buy RTX at 15% and AMAT at 15% to concentrate in the strongest scanner setups.
This plan uses leverage and concentration to stretch for growth, with strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and concentration. It will amplify both upside and drawdown, and leveraged ETFs decay daily. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the significantly higher risk of rapid capital loss.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and reduce overall beta.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell CRWD and SMH to reduce high-beta single names and sector exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize income and monthly distributions.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into a more stable sector.
This plan focuses on generating steady income with a low drawdown profile.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly yield by rotating into income-focused ETFs and stable sectors. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable distributions over growth. Remember, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs; this is not a savings account.
